display: HEALTHY — All signals within research-validated healthy bounds. No action needed.
classification_reason: All signals within research-validated healthy bounds. No action needed.

cyclomatic_complexity.source: McCabe 1976, IEEE Trans. Software Eng. — CC as count of linearly independent paths. rubik/radon official docs — Grade scale A=1-5, B=6-10, C=11-20, D=21-30, E=31-40, F=41+.

maintainability_index.source: Coleman et al., IEEE Computer 1994 — MI formula documented.
maintainability_index.caveat: Van Deursen 2014 — 'Think Twice Before Using MI.' Experimental metric, pair with CC.

code_churn.label: stable
code_churn.source: Munson & Elbaum, IEEE 1998 — Code churn validated as fault surrogate.

bus_factor.source: Spadini et al., ESEC/FSE 2018 — PyDriller process metrics. Minor contributor = <5% lines.

dora.source: DORA 2024 (Google, 39,000+ respondents) — 5 metrics, 4-tier benchmark bands. DORA 2025 (5,000 respondents) — 7 archetypes replacing 4-tier model.

methodology_note: Classification is signal-level, not composite-score-based. No universal composite bands exist in peer-reviewed literature. Each flag traces to a specific published threshold. See CodeScene/Tornhill for the only calibrated code health scale (1-10, healthy >= 9.0).
